rulings.Case Title: ENTREPRENEUR MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. SCOTT SMITH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Complainant Scott Smiths Motion And Notice Of Motion To Vacate Protective Order, Or, In The Alternative, Sever Roe Defendants Pursuant To C.C.P. 1048b
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 11, 2018, Line 4, CROSS COMPLAINANT SCOTT SMITH's Motion To Vacate Protective Order, Or, In The Alternative, Sever Roe Defendants Pursuant To C.C.P. 1048b. Cross-complainant Scott Smith's motion to vacate protective order and to serve a subpoena on Google to determine the true identity of the person(s) sending emails under the name of James Johnson is granted. The order filed September 4, 2018 granting cross-defendant Entrepreneur Media, Inc.'s ex parte motion for a protective order is now vacated and Mr. Smith is granted permission to take all necessary steps to serve his proposed subpoena on Google. Even if this motion were construed as a motion for reconsideration of the order filed September 4, 2018 and did not comply with the requirements of CCP 1008, the court treats this motion as an invitation to reconsider the September 4, 2018 per Le Francois v. Goel (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 1094 and accepts the invitation. Mr. Smith has shown good cause for the service of the subpoena on Google. Because the subpoena seeks to learn the identity of the Roe cross-defendants to enable Mr. Smith to prosecute his cross-claims against the Roe cross-defendants and is not sought to pursue any claims against EMI, the subpoena is not barred by the pendency of the appeal of this court's order granting EMI's anti-SLAPP motion as to Mr. Smith's cross-claims against EMI or the statute barring discovery against a party who filed an anti-SLAPP motion. No ruling by the Court of Appeal will affect Mr. Smith's cross-claims against the Roe cross-defendants and thus the subpoena is not embraced by the pending appeal. Notwithstanding EMI's prior evidentiary submissions denying any connection to the emails sent under the name of James Johnson, the possibility that the subpoena to Google may reveal information that might be relevant to Mr. Smith's cross-claims against EMI in the event that Mr. Smith is ever permitted to pursue those cross-claims does not make the subpoena impermissible at this time. Mr. Smith has shown good cause for the service of the subpoena on Google and none of EMI's arguments opposing the service of the subpoena are persuasive. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Mr. Smith is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: DALE DUNCAN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Marta Munoz Mendoza'S Motion / Notice Of Motion For Charging Orders, Assignment Orders, Restraining Order, And For Appointment Of Receiver (On Judicial Foreclosure Of Two Properties Held By Debtor Anne Kihagi'S Llc: Xelan Prop I, Llc] (Per Judge Kahn On 11/28/18)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 11, 2018, Line 12, Marta Munoz Mendoza'S Motion For Charging Orders, Assignment Orders, Restraining Order, And For Appointment Of Receiver (On Judicial Foreclosure Of Two Properties Held By Debtor Anne Kihagi'S Llc: Xelan Prop I, Llc] (Per Judge Kahn On 11/28/18). Plaintiffs Dale Duncan and Marta Mendoza's motion for charging order, assignment order, restraining order and for appointment of receiver is granted. Plaintiffs have shown good cause for all the relief they seek. Plaintiffs have a substantial judgment in their favor and have not yet received any money from any of the defendants toward satisfaction of that judgment. Since there is no stay regarding judgment collection efforts, there is no persuasive reason why plaintiffs should be deprived of the ability to access the full range of judgment collection remedies available to them under California law. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, recites all the relief sought by the motion and states that all such relief is granted and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: DALE DUNCAN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ms. Kihagi'S Motion To Seal Any Charging Order By The Court.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 11, 2018, Line 12, Defendant Ms. Kihagi'S Motion To Seal Any Charging Order By The Court. Judgment debtor Anne Kihagi's motion to seal any issued charging orders is denied. The right of public access to court records is at its greatest with regard to court orders. A transparent and fully accountable court system cannot function if court orders were not accessible to the public. Ms. Kihagi has failed to show any of the facts required by CRC 2.550(d) for the issuance of the order she seeks. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES PITTS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, HUMAN SERVICES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition And Order Issuing Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 11, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and EPISCOPAL COMMUNITY SERVICES OF SAN FRANCISCO 's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition And Order Issuing Sanctions Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. No later than 12/14/18, Plaintiff shall provide Defendant with 3 days that he's available for a deposition before 12/28/18. If Plaintiff complies with this order, and attends a deposition as agreed upon, sanctions are denied. If Plaintiff does not comply, sanctions in the amount of $100 are awarded to Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ADOLFO ORTEGA VS. PLANT CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, L.P. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Plant Construction Company L.P. And Msp Property , Llc'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Demand For Exchange Of Expert Information, Or In The Alternative, For Relief Pursuant To Ccp Section 473(B)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 11, 2018, Line 15, Defendant Plant Construction Company L.P. And Msp Property , Llc'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Demand For Exchange Of Expert Information, Or In The Alternative, For Relief Pursuant To Ccp Section 473(B). Defendant Plant Construction Company, LP's motion to compel compliance with demand for exchange of expert information is granted. The parties shall exchange expert information on December 13, 2018 or such other day that they mutually agree. The deadline to submit a supplemental expert witness list is December 31, 2018 or such other day that the parties mutually agree. Absent stipulation of the parties, all other discovery deadlines are unaffected by this order. Plant has shown good cause for relief for its counsel's mistake regarding service of the expert demand. Granting the motion is consistent with the purposes of the Discovery Act. Plaintiff Adolfo Ortega's assertion that he will suffer legal prejudice by the granting of the motion is unpersuasive. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: JASMINE JACKSON VS. DIY CO., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Jasmine Jackson'S Ntc Of Mtn And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Nomnomnow, Inc. And Back To Roots, Inc.S Production Of Business Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 11, 2018, Line 4, Jasmine Jackson'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Nomnomnow, Inc. And Back To Roots, Inc.S Production Of Business Records Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Motion to quash denied. While under certain circumstances the information to be obtained via the subpoenas may be considered character evidence, it also may be used for other purposes including impeachment, establishment of habits and pattern of practices etc. It will be up to the trial judge to determine whether any of this information is eventually allowed into evidence. The Court is willing to consider allowing Plaintiff a "first look" of the documents that will be produced. Any documents that Plaintiff objects to would then be reviewed by the Court in-camera. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
